it_montepulciano_boscarelli_vino_nobile.appearance: Medium ruby with garnet reflections developing with age; good clarity and brightness; medium viscosity with slow-forming legs indicating moderate alcohol and extract
it_montepulciano_boscarelli_vino_nobile.nose_summary: Complex and inviting aromatic profile of sour cherry, marasca cherry, ripe red fruits and redcurrant, with roses, violets, and sweet spices. Underpinned by tobacco, chocolate, licorice, and dried herb nuances from oak aging. A pinch of sweetness and floral precision; in youth can be somewhat closed and reserved
it_montepulciano_boscarelli_vino_nobile.palate_summary: Elegant and harmonious with silky, smoothly polished tannins. Cherry flavour at the core with spiciness and a subtle background sweetness. Dense yet juicy with pure, fresh fruit. Riper and fleshier than Chianti Classico on average, but more restrained than Brunello. Mineral and floral nuances emerge on the finish
it_montepulciano_boscarelli_vino_nobile.structural_summary: Acidity: high, fresh and lively. Tannins: medium-plus, silky to firm depending on age, well-polished. Body: medium-plus. Alcohol: 13.5-14%. Finish: very long and persistent with mineral-floral precision. Built for medium to long aging

it_etna_terre_nere_etna_rosso.appearance: Pale to medium ruby, bright and translucent with notable clarity; lighter in hue than most southern Italian reds, often mistaken for Pinot Noir or Nebbiolo on appearance alone; low to medium viscosity reflecting moderate alcohol and elegance
it_etna_terre_nere_etna_rosso.nose_summary: Bright cherry fruit, wild strawberry, and tart red berry aromas with pronounced floral notes of violets and roses. Distinctive volcanic smoke and graphite minerality, with hints of white pepper, dusty strawberry, and herbs. Earthy, mineral, and clean with remarkable purity. Some vintages show blood orange and cranberry notes
it_etna_terre_nere_etna_rosso.palate_summary: Medium-bodied with vibrant, tart red fruit flavours including red cherry, blood orange, and wild strawberry. Salty mineral undertones and iron-graphite character from volcanic soils. Herbal notes and a sweet-and-sour tension. Silky to fine-grained texture with excellent purity and vivid expression. Can show a Barolo-like or Burgundian complexity with age
it_etna_terre_nere_etna_rosso.structural_summary: Acidity: high, vibrant and driving. Tannins: fine and persistent, can saturate deeply in youth with bitter cranberry-cherry pit finish. Body: medium. Alcohol: 13-14.5%. Finish: long with lingering iron-mineral, herbal, and tart red berry notes. Ages gracefully despite apparent delicacy

fr_cote_rotie_guigal_brune_et_blonde.appearance: Dark ruby red to inky purple in colour, opaque to mostly opaque with bright rim; high viscosity with pronounced legs reflecting 14% alcohol and extract from long oak aging
fr_cote_rotie_guigal_brune_et_blonde.nose_summary: Classic Cote-Rotie profile of red and black raspberries, boysenberry, and blackberry with ground black pepper, violet, candied flowers, and spice. Smoky, gently herbal nuances with hints of licorice, olive paste, smoked/cured meat, pencil shavings, and subtle oak-derived vanilla. Some vintages show cassis, black olive, earth, and dark chocolate. The Viognier co-ferment adds a floral lift and aromatic complexity
fr_cote_rotie_guigal_brune_et_blonde.palate_summary: Medium to full-bodied with supple, round tannins softened by long oak aging. Juicy red and blue fruit preserves, raspberry, blackberry, and vanilla core with spicecake, olive paste, and violet pastille flavours. Balanced and satisfying with excellent depth. Concentrated yet not heavy, with underlying char and savoury earthiness. Some vintages show a leaner, more elegant profile while others are richer and more concentrated
fr_cote_rotie_guigal_brune_et_blonde.structural_summary: Acidity: medium-plus, well-integrated and scarcely perceptible. Tannins: medium to medium-plus, round and velvety with slow-building grip from 38 months in 50% new French oak. Body: medium to full. Alcohol: 13-14%. Finish: very long, silky, and persistent with lingering floral, spice and savoury notes. Excellent aging potential of 10-20+ years

fr_chateauneuf_du_pape_beaucastel_rouge.appearance: Deep ruby-red to ruby-purple, with carmine highlights in youth; excellent clarity despite no fining; medium-plus viscosity reflecting full body and 14.5% alcohol. Shiny and bright
fr_chateauneuf_du_pape_beaucastel_rouge.nose_summary: Powerful, complex, and elegant with superb aromatic depth. Classic Beaucastel signature of dark wild berries, cassis, cherry puree, kirsch, and plum reduction interwoven with garrigue, dried herbs, smoked meat, black pepper, and leather. Secondary notes of cocoa, chocolate, tobacco, black licorice, roasted herbs, potpourri, and warm stone. The Mourvedre dominance gives a distinctive savoury, meaty, peppery character that differentiates it from Grenache-led CdPs. Violet and floral top notes from the diverse 13-variety assemblage
fr_chateauneuf_du_pape_beaucastel_rouge.palate_summary: Full-bodied and silky with dense, perfectly balanced structure. Generous and seamless with palate-staining blackcurrant, cherry-cola, and sweet red fruit flavours. Spicecake, leather, alder, and dried garrigue build with aeration. The Mourvedre contributes mouthcoating, deep tannins with plenty of flesh. Shows remarkable purity and clarity despite power. Energetic and impressively long with a sleek iron spine. Elegant texture with sweet tannins and great complexity
fr_chateauneuf_du_pape_beaucastel_rouge.structural_summary: Acidity: medium-plus, lively and fresh. Tannins: ripe, fine-grained, and structured; chewy in youth but velvety with age from foudre aging (no new oak). Body: full. Alcohol: 14.5%. Finish: very long and harmonious with lingering warm stone, spice, and sweet dark berry notes. Exceptional aging potential of 20+ years. Built to last

fr_cotes_de_provence_esclans_whispering_.appearance: Very pale salmon-pink to pale golden pink, almost between a white wine and a rose in colour; brilliant clarity; translucent with notable legs indicating moderate alcohol (13%). The benchmark for pale Provence rose colour
fr_cotes_de_provence_esclans_whispering_.nose_summary: Fresh, fruity, and immediately appealing with strawberry, raspberry, red berry, and subtle citrus (lemon zest, pink grapefruit, lime). Delicate peach, white cherry, and watermelon rind nuances. Floral notes of peony and white flowers. Hints of salty minerality and a touch of herbal character. Clean and aromatic without excessive intensity; more perfumed than pungent
fr_cotes_de_provence_esclans_whispering_.palate_summary: Sleek, dry, and crisp with mellow citrus and young strawberry flavours at the core. Red berry fruit from old-vine Grenache with subtle lees-derived creaminess and silky mouthfeel. Plump yet balanced with succulent lemony acidity. Light-bodied and refreshing with a seamless, soothing character. Tart pink grapefruit and subtle minerality add interest. Accessible and immediately enjoyable
fr_cotes_de_provence_esclans_whispering_.structural_summary: Acidity: medium-plus, crisp and fresh. Tannins: negligible. Body: light. Alcohol: 13%. Finish: medium, dry and lip-smacking with lingering fruit and a hint of salty minerality. Stainless steel only, no oak influence. Designed for immediate drinking within 1-2 years of vintage

fr_bandol_tempier_cuvee_classique.appearance: Deep ruby-red to dark ruby with good concentration; can appear deceptively light on pour before revealing depth; medium-plus viscosity. Bright and clear despite no fining or filtration
fr_bandol_tempier_cuvee_classique.nose_summary: Rich and complex with dark berry fruit (blackberry, boysenberry, black cherry) layered with Mourvedre-typical reduction that can present as smoky, earthy, and feral in youth. Tobacco, old leather, wet autumn leaves, iron, and worn leather. Garrigue, dried herbs, violet, and spice box notes. A distinctive iodine-saline character from Bandol's maritime terroir. Can show a seductive prettiness with violet and graphite shavings alongside crunchy red plum. The Mourvedre funk evolves and integrates with bottle age
fr_bandol_tempier_cuvee_classique.palate_summary: Robust yet refined with layers of black cherry, dark cherry, plum, and boysenberry. The Grenache adds sweetness while Cinsault and Carignan provide rustic complexity. Earthy Mourvedre undertones with soy, iron, and leather flavours. Medium-weight and broad in good vintages, with a silk to grippy texture. Salt and bitterness on the finish in youth that resolves with age. Dark, dense, and powerful with a distinctive Bandol iodine-leather signature that distinguishes it from all other Mourvedre-based wines
fr_bandol_tempier_cuvee_classique.structural_summary: Acidity: medium-plus. Tannins: firm and grippy in youth, dense and structured; can be tannic and brooding when young, requiring significant age to soften. Body: full. Alcohol: 14-14.5%. Finish: long and satisfying with hints of spice, mineral, and lingering dark fruit. Best with 8-12+ years of age; the Classique is the most approachable of the Tempier range but still built for the long haul. No new oak (foudre-aged 18-20 months)

fr_morgon_foillard_cote_du_py.appearance: Medium ruby to medium-deep ruby, bright and clear; lighter than most reds but deeper than village Beaujolais. Good translucency reflecting the Gamay grape and whole-cluster vinification. Moderate viscosity
fr_morgon_foillard_cote_du_py.nose_summary: Wonderfully complex with exquisite delineation: wild berries, dark cherry, raspberry, and crushed stone. Violets and subtle floral notes with hints of ash, cracked black pepper, and light stony/granite minerality. Some farmyard character (from natural winemaking) alongside sweet-and-sour red and black cherry. Spicy notes and a mineral-laden backbone. Can show reduction in youth that blows off. No bubblegum or banana esters typical of commercial Beaujolais
fr_morgon_foillard_cote_du_py.palate_summary: Deep, structured, and complex with a velvety lushness that makes it irresistible when young. Silky yet structured with juicy red and black fruit wrapped around a core of iron-granite minerality. Sapid and vivacious with filigree tannins and a silver thread of acidity. Plump and juicy dark cherry and raspberry fruit with savoury earth, sweet-and-sour tension, and wild herb complexity. Develops Burgundian complexity with time. Rustic structure beneath the elegant exterior
fr_morgon_foillard_cote_du_py.structural_summary: Acidity: medium-plus, brisk and fresh with great balance. Tannins: fine to medium, well-structured but silky, not aggressive. Body: medium to medium-plus. Alcohol: 13-13.5%. Finish: long and elegant with lingering freshness, savory earth, cracked pepper, and mineral persistence. Ages beautifully for 5-15 years despite accessible youth. No new oak (neutral used Burgundy barrels, 6-9 months)

fr_beaujolais_villages_duboeuf.appearance: Brilliant garnet to purple-red, light in body but deeper in colour than expected; good clarity and brightness. Light viscosity with quickly falling tears reflecting lower alcohol and light extract
fr_beaujolais_villages_duboeuf.nose_summary: Textbook semi-carbonic Beaujolais character with incomparable fruitiness in the purest Gamay expression. Strawberry, raspberry, and banana aromas (strawberry-banana jello quality from carbonic maceration esters). Black fruits and creme de cassis with cherry and blueberry notes. Some floral character. Intensity is moderate and immediately fruit-forward. A prototypical Beaujolais-Villages aromatic profile with hints of pomegranate
fr_beaujolais_villages_duboeuf.palate_summary: Juicy sweet berry fruit dominated by strawberry, red plum, and red cherry flavours. Round and vinous with soft, delicate tannins contributing to a smooth, silky texture. Light-bodied and very easy to drink. Simple fruit-forward character without complexity. Barely medium-bodied with supple tannins and generous fruit. Harmonious, moderately dry finish that keeps giving fruit. Confected berry quality with surprising structure for the price tier
fr_beaujolais_villages_duboeuf.structural_summary: Acidity: medium, adequate but not driving. Tannins: low, very soft and smooth. Body: light to medium. Alcohol: 12.5-13%. Finish: medium-short to medium, fruity and clean. Harmonious and complete but without depth or complexity. Designed for immediate drinking within 1-2 years. Stainless steel, no oak

fr_champagne_pol_roger_brut_reserve.appearance: Beautiful golden straw to pale gold with silver reflections; brilliant clarity. Abundant, fine, and persistent mousse with elegant bead. Creamy effervescence typical of long lees aging (4+ years)
fr_champagne_pol_roger_brut_reserve.nose_summary: Powerful and attractive with aromas of ripe pear, yellow apple, mango, and cooked pip fruit (quince jelly, apricot). Light flavours of honeysuckle, white jasmine, and citrus blossom. Brioche, toast, vanilla, and beeswax notes from extended lees contact. Hints of mandarin oil, honeycomb, elderflower, fresh bread, and musky peach. Can show Amalfi lemon, rhubarb, and anise. Clear, precise, and fresh with both fruit and autolytic complexity
fr_champagne_pol_roger_brut_reserve.palate_summary: Medium-bodied with a deliciously fine and creamy mousse. Round and fruit-driven with golden apple, honeycomb, nectarine, and poached pear flavours. Lemon zest, candied orange peel, and ginger nuances. Cooked fruit qualities (quince, apricot jam) mingle with beeswax and acacia honey. Charming and perfectly balanced with pleasant freshness and underlying structure. Fleshy and enveloping with a generous core of fruit, lively acids, and a pillowy mousse. Red-fruited undertones from the Pinot Noir component
fr_champagne_pol_roger_brut_reserve.structural_summary: Acidity: high, dynamic and fresh. Tannins: n/a (sparkling). Body: medium. Alcohol: 12.5%. Mousse: fine, creamy, and persistent. Dosage: 8-10 g/L (Brut). Finish: long-lasting and aromatic with both fruity (candied citrus, tangerine) and spicy (cardamom, anise) notes; steely and mineral on the close. Elegant and refined with taut persistence. Equal thirds Pinot Noir/Meunier/Chardonnay with 25% reserve wines
